Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Although this patch works, it breaks the interface API as James mentioned. However I think there is a better way to skin that cat. If you use a combination of (pid, which lustre fs, lk_wfd), you can uniquely identify the connection that has to be closed. The current code in liblustreapi_hsm.c set lk_wfd to -1 before closing, so that can be interpreted as close any that matches the pid for that lustre fs. Otherwise only close the one that match. Only a minor change should be done in userspace to not set lk_wfd to -1. Unless an existing application opens twice the same filesystem, that should be compatible with existing compiled applications.

Hi Frank,
the main part of patch set 4 is adding the test for "libcfs_kkuc_group_rem(0, KUC_GRP_HSM, -1,  NULL);"
in mdc_precleanup, which is called to make sure the the registered "kernelcomm"s to be cleaned up during
umounting Lustre. what kind of test do you prefer to adding for it, thanks!

Thanks for reminding me. That one felt through the cracks.

libcfs_kkuc_group_rem can have rfd=-1, which I assume means everything. But there is no test for that value. Is that what the test uid==0 for? And wouldn't that test always be true as long as root unregisters a channel?

Patch Set 8:

Can we please get a review from HSM-savvy folks on this.

Will this break the userspace API or ABI or are the changes internal to the kernel and transparent to liblustreapi?

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

This is a strange way to fix the memory leak. I think a better way would be to remove the pdata parameter from libcfs_kkuc_group_rem(). It serves no purpose and the way we handle it causes the leak. Just free all of the kkuc_regs in the group if the uid is 0.

parents.subject: LU-6978 utils: mkfs.lustre to recognise non ldiskfs opts

commit.subject: LU-6485 libcfs: embed kr_data into kkuc_reg
commit.message:
LU-6485 libcfs: embed kr_data into kkuc_reg

In struct kkuc_reg, the "kr_data" is difficult to be freed
outside of libcfs, then it's better to change it to be
inline data instead of the data pointer.

Change-Id: Iaf5e9fbb9bad2540f51da2c4fd9c4047640d0877
